title: Jacketed polymeric pipe with integrated weight
abstract: A submergible flexible pipe and a method of making a submergible flexible pipe are disclosed. The submergible flexible pipe includes a hose having one or more layers and an extruded jacket encasing the hose. The jacket is formed from a polymeric material and a filler so that the flexible pipe with the fluid to be conveyed therein is negatively buoyant in fresh water and/or sea water.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A submergible flexible pipe for conveying a fluid comprising: a hose having one or more layers; and an extruded jacket encasing the hose, the jacket comprising a polymeric material and a filler having a sufficient density and volume so that the flexible pipe with the fluid to be conveyed therein is negatively buoyant. 2. The pipe of claim 1, wherein the hose includes a core tube resistant to petroleum materials. 3. The pipe of claim 2, wherein the core tube is an extruded polymeric material. 4. The pipe of claim 2, wherein the hose includes a braided reinforcement layer formed around a circumference of the core tube. 5. The pipe of claim 4, wherein the braided reinforcement is formed from a plurality of intertwined strands formed of at least one of aramid, carbon, polyester, or fiberglass fibers. 6. The pipe of claim 4, wherein the jacket is extruded over the braided reinforcement and the core tube. 7. The pipe of claim 6, wherein the jacket is resistant to environmental attack. 8. The pipe of claim 6, wherein the jacket is impervious to seawater. 9. The pipe of claim 1, wherein the filler is a copper-based alloy. 10. The pipe of claim 9, wherein the copper-based alloy is powdered bronze. 11. The pipe of claim 1, wherein the filler is barium sulfate. 12. A submergible flexible pipe for submarine conveyance of petroleum materials, comprising: a hose having: an extruded core tube resistant to petroleum materials; a braided reinforcement layer formed around a circumference of the core tube; and an extruded jacket encasing the reinforcement layer and the core tube, wherein the jacket comprises a polymer and a filler having a specific gravity greater than 5 so that the flexible pipe in combination with the petroleum materials being conveyed therein is negatively buoyant in water. 13. A method of making a submergible flexible pipe, the method comprising: extruding a core tube; forming a braided reinforcement layer around a circumference of the core tube; and extruding a jacket encasing the braided reinforcement layer and the core tube, the jacket including a mixture of polymer and a filler so that the flexible pipe is negatively buoyant. 14. The method of claim 13, further comprising coiling the flexible pipe. 15. The method of claim 14 wherein the flexible pipe is coiled onto a spool.
